WTI Crude Rises on Surprise Inventory Draw

Published: September 25, 2024

WTI crude oil futures surged more than 8% on Wednesday after the U.S. Energy Information Administration reported a larger-than-expected draw in crude inventories.

The EIA said commercial crude stockpiles fell by 4.5 million barrels last week, well above analyst expectations for a decline of about 1.2 million barrels.

WTI for November delivery settled at $72.40 per barrel, up $5.60, or 8.4%.
